summary: Moved Peach FloatFall routines out of ftPe_Init.s into a new C translation unit, partially matching the ftPe_FloatFall action callbacks and helper logic while leaving the harder ftPe_8011BE80 path guarded with inline asm for MUST_MATCH. The PR also introduced a Peach-specific motion-vars union, refined fighter input and motion-id types, added needed ft prototypes, and updated obj_files.mk so the new source participates in the build.
what_changed: Deleted the ftPe_FloatFall_Anim, ftPe_FloatFall_IASA, ftPe_FloatFall_Phys, ftPe_FloatFall_Coll, ftPe_8011BE80, and ftPe_8011BF34 assembly bodies from asm/melee/ft/chara/ftPe_Init.s.

Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftPeach/ftPe_FloatFall.c with C implementations for the simple FloatFall callbacks: Anim checks ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining and calls ft_800CCDA8, IASA is empty, Phys calls ft_800CC6C8, and Coll calls ft_800831CC with ft_80096CC8 and ft_80082B1C.

Added a partially matched C version of ftPe_8011BE80 that reads GET_FIGHTER(gobj), checks fp->input.x668 & HSD_Pad_A or ft_800DF478(fp), tests fp->fv.pe.x4 > 0, handles a held item case with it_8026B30C == 3 and ft_800CDDA0, otherwise calls ftPe_8011BF34.

Kept ftPe_8011BE80 under a MUST_MATCH/WIP split: the non-WIP MUST_MATCH path uses inline asm with nofralloc and explicit labels, while the C path includes a documented unused stack workaround under MUST_MATCH.
Implemented static ftPe_8011BF34 in C, setting fp->mv.pe.floatfall.x0 to 0 and changing motion via ft_8008CFAC(gobj, msid + ftCo_MS_CaptureDamageKoopa).
Added ftPe_FloatFall.h declarations and moved the ftPe_8011BE80 declaration out of ftPe_Float.h into the new header.
Included ftPe_FloatFall.h from ftPe_Float.c, indicating existing Float code now depends on the FloatFall helper declaration.
Added the new ftPe_FloatFall.c object to obj_files.mk before the remaining ftPe_Init.s object.
Added union ftPe_MotionVars with a floatfall struct at fp+2340 containing int x0, and inserted union ftPe_MotionVars pe into Fighter::mv.

Introduced typedef enum_t FtMotionId in ft/forward.h, changed Fighter.motion_id to FtMotionId, changed ft_8008CE68 to return FtMotionId, and updated a Game & Watch aerial local variable from enum_t to FtMotionId.
Typed Fighter input fields x65C_heldInputs and x668 as HSD_Pad instead of raw integer types, enabling named pad-bit checks such as HSD_Pad_A.
Renamed the moved sdata2 constants for 0.0 and 1.0 from local asm labels .L_804D97D0/.L_804D97D4 to C-visible lbl_804D97D0/lbl_804D97D4 and updated remaining asm references accordingly.
Fixed an unrelated offset comment in ftCaptain/types.h: speciallw.x2 is at fp+2342, not fp+2340.
smart_moves: Separated the easy exact C matches from the difficult helper instead of blocking the whole FloatFall extraction; this reduced asm while preserving a MUST_MATCH inline-asm fallback for the stubborn function.
Created a dedicated ftPe_FloatFall.c/.h pair rather than continuing to accumulate Peach FloatFall code in ftPe_Init.s or ftPe_Float.h, improving organization around the action state.
Used fighter-specific motion vars, fp->mv.pe.floatfall.x0, instead of leaving fp+2340 as an untyped raw store, making the ftPe_8011BF34 behavior reusable for future Peach motion work.

Introduced FtMotionId as a semantic alias for motion-state IDs and propagated it through ft_8008CE68 and Fighter.motion_id, improving type intent without changing the underlying enum_t representation.
Typed input bitfields as HSD_Pad so checks like fp->input.x668 & HSD_Pad_A become self-documenting instead of magic integer masks.
Preserved sdata2 constant addresses by defining lbl_804D97D0 and lbl_804D97D4 in the new C file and updating remaining asm references to the new symbol names.
decomp_lessons: When a contiguous asm region contains both simple callbacks and one stubborn function, extract the callbacks and use a conditional inline-asm bridge for the unmatched helper; partial file progress is still valuable.
For motion-state decompilation, model fp+2340 through the fighter-specific motion-vars union as soon as a store is understood, even if the field name remains generic.
Semantic typedefs such as FtMotionId can clarify APIs like ft_8008CE68 and local variables without requiring the full enum to be known.
Replacing raw input integers with pad flag types unlocks readable bit checks and helps future reviewers verify controller-related branches.
When moving assembly constants into C, update remaining asm users to reference the new lbl_* symbols so object layout and relocations stay consistent.
Inline asm guarded by MUST_MATCH can coexist with a readable C implementation under non-strict builds, and an explicit comment like '@todo Unused stack' records the remaining matching issue.
naming_conventions: New Peach FloatFall files use the fighter/action naming pattern ftPe_FloatFall.c and ftPe_FloatFall.h.
Action callback names follow the established suffixes ftPe_FloatFall_Anim, ftPe_FloatFall_IASA, ftPe_FloatFall_Phys, and ftPe_FloatFall_Coll.
Unknown but address-identified helpers retain address-based names such as ftPe_8011BE80 and ftPe_8011BF34.
C-visible float constants are named lbl_804D97D0 and lbl_804D97D4 rather than local asm-style .L_ labels.
Fighter-specific motion vars are grouped as union ftPe_MotionVars and accessed through fp->mv.pe.floatfall.
Motion IDs are represented with the semantic alias FtMotionId rather than plain enum_t where evidence indicates motion-state use.
Controller input fields use HSD_Pad-typed names such as x65C_heldInputs and x668, with named flags like HSD_Pad_A.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed matched assembly from ftPe_Init.s while leaving later unrelated Peach assembly in the same file.
Used `#if defined(MUST_MATCH) && !defined(WIP)` to select an inline assembly implementation of ftPe_8011BE80 for strict matching.
Used `#pragma push`, `asm bool ftPe_8011BE80(HSD_GObj*)`, `nofralloc`, and explicit `lbl_8011...` labels to preserve the original codegen for the unmatched helper.
Provided a C implementation of ftPe_8011BE80 in the non-inline-asm branch for readability and future matching work.
Added an unused stack byte array under MUST_MATCH in the C branch to document and potentially influence stack layout, but the PR still title-labels the work as only partial matching.
Kept constants as static float const symbols in the C file and adjusted remaining asm references from .L_804D97D0/.L_804D97D4 to lbl_804D97D0/lbl_804D97D4.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review records were present in the provided PR context.
The only human-text body evidence was an embedded image; no textual rationale beyond the title was available.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that fully match ftPe_8011BE80 or remove its MUST_MATCH inline asm fallback.
Search for subsequent cleanup of duplicate ft_800CC6C8 prototypes in ft_0877.h.
Look for later renames of ftPe_8011BF34 or ftPe_MotionVars.floatfall.x0 once the field purpose became known.
Check whether FtMotionId was expanded from a typedef alias into a real enum in later fighter decomp work.
Search later Peach PRs for continued extraction of ftPe_Init.s after ftPe_FloatFall.c was added.
